LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OF PLAINTIFF RANDOLPH ANDERSON'S SOLE CAUSE OF ACTION OF PREMISES LIABILITY IS DENIED. THE COURT GRANTS THE REQUEST TO JUDICIALLY NOTICE THE COMPLAINT. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING) COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/CVA)

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
EILEEN TSATURYAN, ESQ, DOWNTOWN LA LAW GROUP (213) 723-2122 Igor@downtownlalaw.com Attorney for Plaintiff, RANDOLPH ANDERSON
Christopher A. Nolin, Esq., GORDON-CREED, KELLEY, HOLL & SUGERMAN, LLP (415) 421-3100 nolin@gkhs.com Attorneys for Defendant City of San Francisco and County of San Francisco

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO , ERRONEOUSLY SUED SEPARATELY AS CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary judgment of Plaintiff Randolph Anderson's sole cause of action of premises liability is denied. The court grants the request to judicially notice the complaint.

(SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
